commands.review_summary:
The branch filters waited A2A reply extraction away from transcript-only assistant rows, message-tool mirrors, and projected inter-session input rows, adjusts same-session announce handling, and adds focused QA/mock-provider regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +110, Tests +652, Other +141. Total +903 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can expose message-tool mirrors and projected sessions_send inter-session rows as assistant history, while run-wait reads latest assistant text without filtering them. I did not run a live transport reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed because the code review is clean but the external real-behavior proof gate cannot be satisfied by an automated repair worker.

target.title: fix: A2A handoffs can duplicate message-tool replies
target.body:
Related: #39476
Related: #91161
Related: #93293

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users asking an agent to hand work to another session could receive duplicate visible output when the target session replied through `message(action="send")` and the waited A2A path later treated transcript bookkeeping as a fresh assistant reply.

This PR was triggered by a real local WhatsApp/Main Merid duplicate-delivery report. The private message content is intentionally not included, but the observed shape was: one correct requester-visible reply, followed by a second visible message derived from the same A2A/source-reply flow.

This is a narrow companion to #39476. It does not replace #91161's reverse `sessions_send` tool guard, and it does not change compaction replay behavior covered by #93293.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix keeps waited A2A reply extraction on model-authored assistant replies instead of transcript artifacts. It excludes inter-session input rows, raw OpenClaw delivery mirrors, projected message-tool mirrors, and Gateway-injected assistant rows before they can feed ping-pong or announce delivery.

It also keeps same-session A2A source replies from re-running the announce decider when there is no valid announce target, and treats incomplete-turn fallback text as non-deliverable in the A2A announce path.

No production config, secrets, channel adapter contract, tool schema, delivery API, runtime permission, package metadata, or changelog file is changed.

## User Impact

A2A handoffs that reply visibly through the message tool no longer turn already-delivered source replies or forwarded `sessions_send` transcript rows into a second visible message. Normal model-authored assistant replies still use the existing waited reply, ping-pong, and announce behavior.

## Evidence

Redacted real Gateway/channel proof using the repo QA suite:

```text
OPENCLAW_HOME="$PWD/.artifacts/openclaw-home-pr97259" \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$PWD/.artifacts/openclaw-state-pr97259" \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$PWD/.artifacts/openclaw-state-pr97259/openclaw.json" \
pnpm openclaw qa suite --provider-mode mock-openai --transport qa-channel \
  --scenario a2a-message-tool-mirror-dedupe \
  --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/a2a-message-tool-mirror-dedupe-pr97259-v9-clean

Passed: 1
Failed: 0
```

The QA scenario runs a real Gateway child with `qa-channel` and `mock-openai`, then proves:

```text
outbound=group:qa-a2a-mirror-room:QA-A2A-MESSAGE-TOOL-MIRROR-OK
sourceTool={"sessionKey":"agent:qa:qa-channel:group:group:qa-a2a-mirror-room","message":"qa group visible reply tool check. Use the visible room reply path. exact marker: `QA-A2A-MESSAGE-TOOL-MIRROR-OK`","timeoutSeconds":0}
targetTool={"action":"send","message":"QA-A2A-MESSAGE-TOOL-MIRROR-OK"}
duplicateWindowMs=8000
```

That gives one source `sessions_send`, one target `message(action="send")`, and exactly one requester-visible marker after the duplicate window.

Focused regression tests:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/run-wait.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server.sessions-send.test.ts \
  extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-catalog.test.ts

Test Files  2 passed (2) in gateway shard
Tests       10 passed (10)

Test Files  2 passed (2) in agents shard
Tests       54 passed (54)

Test Files  1 passed (1) in extension-qa shard
Tests       30 passed (30)
```

Mock-provider fixture guard:

```text
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-qa.config.ts \
  extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.test.ts \
  --testNamePattern "plans sessions_send for the A2A message-tool mirror proof scenario" \
  --reporter verbose

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       1 passed | 106 skipped (107)
```

Targeted local checks:

```text
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  src/agents/run-wait.ts \
  src/agents/run-wait.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.ts \
  src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts \
  extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.ts \
  extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.test.ts \
  qa/scenarios/channels/a2a-message-tool-mirror-dedupe.yaml
# passed

pnpm exec oxlint \
  src/agents/run-wait.ts \
  src/agents/run-wait.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.ts \
  src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts \
  extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.ts \
  extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.test.ts
# passed

git diff --check
# passed
```

No live production WhatsApp message was sent for this proof. The behavior proof uses the repo's QA Gateway/channel harness with redacted deterministic markers.

AI-assisted: yes. Prepared with Codex, and I understand the code change.
